title: betula × purpusii
intro: Betula × purpusii, also known as Purpurea Birch, is a hybrid of the Betula pendula and Betula nana, resulting in a unique and adaptable tree species. The medicinal properties of Betula × purpusii lie in its bark, which contains salicin, a compound similar to aspirin, providing anti-inflammatory and pain-relieving effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate headaches, and soothe digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Herbal preparations of Betula × purpusii can be found in teas, tinctures, and infusions, and are often used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu.
